questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: An object is placed in a medium of refractive index <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>. An electromagnetic wave of intensity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>8</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> falls normally on the object and it is absorbed completely. The radiation pressure on the object would be (speed of light in free space <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>8</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> ):
questions.solution:
The formula to calculate the radiation pressure is given by

P=Iv   ...1

Also, the velocity of the electromagnetic wave can be expressed as

μ=cv   ...2

Equations (1) and (2) imply that

P=I·μc=6×108×33×108=6 N m-2
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The diameter of a sphere is measured using a vernier caliper whose 9 divisions of main scale are equal to 10 divisions of vernier scale. The shortest division on the main scale is equal to $1 \mathrm{~mm}$. The main scale reading is $2 \mathrm{~cm}$ and second division of vernier scale coincides with a division on main scale. If mass of the sphere is 8.635 g, the density of the sphere is:
questions.solution: Given $9 \mathrm{MSD}=10 \mathrm{VSD}$ $\operatorname{mass}=8.635 \mathrm{~g}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{LC}=1 \mathrm{MSD}-1 \mathrm{VSD} \\ & \mathrm{LC}=1 \mathrm{MSD}-\frac{9}{10} \mathrm{MSD} \\ & \mathrm{LC}=\frac{1}{10} \mathrm{MSD} \\ & \mathrm{LC}=0.01 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$ Reading of diameter $=\mathrm{MSR}+\mathrm{LC} \times \mathrm{VSR}$ $\begin{aligned} & =2 \mathrm{~cm}+(0.01) \times(2) \\ & =2.02 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$ Volume of sphere $=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(\frac{d}{2}\right)^3=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(\frac{2.02}{2}\right)^3$ $=4.32 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ Density $=\frac{\text { mass }}{\text { volume }}=\frac{8.635}{4.32}=1.998 \sim 2.00 \mathrm{~g}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.q: There are 100 divisions on the circular scale of a screw gauge of pitch $1 \mathrm{~mm}$. With no measuring quantity in between the jaws, the zero of the circular scale lies 5 divisions below the reference line. The diameter of a wire is then measured using this screw gauge. It is found that 4 linear scale divisions are clearly visible while 60 divisions on circular scale coincide with the reference line. The diameter of the wire is :
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion(A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A) : In Vernier calliper if positive zero error exists, then while taking measurements, the reading taken will be more than the actual reading.
Reason (R) : The zero error in Vernier Calliper might have happened due to manufacturing defect or due to rough handling.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.solution:
If a positive zero error exists in a vernier callipers, it means that the instrument shows a reading greater than zero even when the jaws are closed and there is no object being measured. In such a case, when actual measurements are taken, the readings will be more than the actual values.

The zero error in Vernier callipers can indeed occur due to manufacturing defects or rough handling. Manufacturing defects may lead to misalignments or inaccuracies in the instrument, while rough handling can result in damage that affects the calibration of the callipers.

Hence, both (A) and (R) are correct, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The measured value of the length of a simple pendulum is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> accuracy. The time for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> oscillations was measured to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn></math> seconds with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> second resolution. From these measurements, the accuracy in the measurement of acceleration due to gravity is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>N</mi><mo>%</mo></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>N</mi></math> is:
questions.solution:
Time period of a simple pendulum is given by, T=2πlg.

⇒g=4π2lT2

Therefore, fractional error will be

Δgg=Δll+2ΔTT

=0.220+2140

=1.220

Percentage change =1.220×100=6%
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A plane EM wave is propagating along $x$ direction. It has a wavelength of $4 \mathrm{~mm}$. If electric field is in $y$ direction with the maximum magnitude of $60 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$, the equation for magnetic field is :
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{E}=\mathrm{BC} \Rightarrow 60=\mathrm{B} \times 3 \times 10^8$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{B}=2 \times 10^{-7}$
Also $\quad \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{f} \lambda$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 3 \times 10^8=\mathrm{f} \times 4 \times 10^{-3} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{f}=\frac{3}{4} \times 10^{11} \\ & \Rightarrow \omega=2 \pi \mathrm{f}=\frac{3}{4} \times 2 \pi \times 10^{11} \\ & \Rightarrow \omega=\frac{\pi}{2} \times 10^3 \mathrm{C}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad$ Electric field $\Rightarrow y$ direction
Propagation $\Rightarrow \mathrm{x}$ direction
Magnetic field $\Rightarrow \mathrm{z}$-direction
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In the given circuit, the breakdown voltage of the Zener diode is 3.0 V. What is the value of Iz ?
questions.solution:
Given that, Vz=3 V

Let potential at B=0 V
Potential at EVE=10 V

Now, VC=VA=3 V and,

Iz+I1=I   ...1

The current through 1 kΩ resistor is given by

I=10-31000 A=71000 A

From the diagram, it follows that

I1=32000 A

Therefore, from equation (1), it follows that

Iz=7-1.51000 A=5.5 mA
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A Zener diode of breakdown voltage 10 V is used as a voltage regulator as shown in the figure. The current through the Zener diode is
questions.solution:
As Zener is in breakdown region, therefore potential drop across it would be 10 V. Now, 

I3=10500=150 A and 

I1=10200=120 A

Also, I2=I1-I3

⇒I2=120-150=3100 A=30 mA
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The output of the given circuit diagram is
questions.solution:
If A=0; A¯=1

A=1; A¯=0

B=0; B¯=1

B=1; B¯=0

Using De Morgan's theorem, we can write 

Y=A+B¯+A¯+B¯=A+B¯¯·A¯+B¯=A¯·B·A·B¯=0
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In the given circuit if the power rating of Zener diode is 10 mW, the value of series resistance Rs to regulate the input unregulated supply is:
questions.solution:
Potential drop across Rs, 

V1=8-5=3 V

Current through the load resistor

I=51×103=5 mA

Maximum current through Zener diode

IZ, max=105=2 mA

And minimum current through Zener diode

IZ, min=0

∴ IS, max=5+2=7mA

And RS, min=V1IS, max=37kΩ

Similarly,

IS, min=5 mA

And RS, max=V1IS, min=35 kΩ

∴ 37kΩ<Rs<35kΩ
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
